fact: March 6 is exactly 65 days into the year, which means there are exactly 300 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, March 6 falls smack in the middle of when maple trees start making their sweet, sticky sap that becomes maple syrup!
The sun rises about 2 minutes earlier each day around March 6 in most places, so you're literally gaining daylight faster than you can blink!
March 6 is when hibernating bears in North America typically start waking up and getting grumpy because they haven't eaten in months!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, March 6 is when the leaves start turning brilliant orange and red as autumn kicks in!
This date falls exactly 2 weeks before the spring equinox, making it the perfect 'almost spring but not quite' day in the north!
Earthworms start wiggling up to the surface around March 6 in temperate climates as the soil begins to thaw and warm up!
March 6 falls during Pisces season in the zodiac calendar, so March 6 babies are swimming with the fishes!
In northern regions, March 6 is when you might hear the first 'crack' of ice breaking up on frozen lakes and rivers!
Many migrating bird species use early March as nature's alarm clock, often starting their epic journeys north within days of March 6 every year!
